papers.title_en: Changing Modalities: Adapting Remote Sensing Models to New Satellites and Sensors
papers.authors: Tim G. Zhou, Anthony Fuller, Geoff Pleiss, Evan Shelhamer
papers.evidence: 处理遥感模型中的模态缺失/增加
papers.tldr: 遥感机器学习模型通常基于固定传感器模态训练，但卫星更新引入新传感器或淘汰旧传感器，导致模态变化需重新标注训练。为此提出DeluluNet，一种模块化架构，通过模态幻觉从现有模态预测缺失模态，结合单模态教师进行无标注多模态学习。该模型能适应模态替换、增加、子集三种场景，仅需少量未标注数据即可保持预测。这为传感器动态变化下的模型复用提供了无需大量重新标注的实用方案。
papers.abstract_en: Machine learning models for remote sensing are trained and deployed on a static set of modalities. However, as we equip newer satellites with novel sensors and retire old ones, practitioners may wish to deploy an existing model on a substitution, superset, or subset of modalities with minimal retraining given data availability or practical computational constraints. We study the setting of updating existing models to changing modalities and identify three main scenarios: Modality Transfer (substitution), Addition (superset), and Peeking (subset). We propose DeluluNet, an architecture with modular components for all three changing modality scenarios. DeluluNet is trained end-to-end, learning a multi-modal model from a unimodal teacher and unlabeled multimodal data via modality hallucination--predicting missing modality representations from those that are present. As a result, DeluluNet can keep predicting even when input modalities change, providing a practical alternative to re-labeling and re-training in a changing world.

papers.title_en: RAPID: A Reproducible Multi-Agent Pipeline for Interpretable Disaster Damage Assessment from Satellite and Street-View Imagery
papers.authors: Yifan Yang, Wenjing Gong, Kaili Zhang, Lei Zou, Zhengzhong Tu, Hao Li, Zongrong Li, Xinyue Ye
papers.evidence: 多模态遥感；卫星与街景图像融合；灾害评估
papers.tldr: 极端气候频发，现有监督学习方法泛化能力差，难以处理多模态地理空间数据。本文提出RAPID，一个可复现的多智能体管线，通过协调专业智能体进行跨视角理解、图像恢复和结构化损伤识别，实现零样本灾害损伤评估。在多种灾害类型评估中，总体准确率达0.92，跨视角损伤严重度预测达0.627。RAPID为自主灾害智能提供了基础框架。
papers.abstract_en: Due to the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme climate events, there is a clear demand for intelligent, scalable, and autonomous approaches to disaster damage assessment. Existing methods, largely based on supervised learning and task-specific fine-tuning, struggle to generalize under domain shifts, long-tailed data distributions, and heterogeneous geospatial data sources, especially in disaster scenarios. They also often lack the ability to integrate and reason across multimodal geospatial information, such as satellite images and street-view images. In this paper, we introduce RAPID, a reproducible multi-agent pipeline for interpretable disaster damage assessment, including damage-level assessment, damage-type interpretation, and actionable suggestions for response, remediation, and recovery. RAPID coordinates specialized agents to perform cross-view understanding, image restoration, structured damage recognition, and geographical reasoning across heterogeneous data modalities. Without task-specific fine-tuning, RAPID supports zero-shot damage assessment by jointly using complementary information from remote sensing and ground-level perspectives. The system produces fine-grained, interpretable assessments and automatically generates location-specific, decision-relevant disaster reports to support early-stage emergency response. We evaluate RAPID across hurricanes, floods, wildfires, and earthquakes using multiple cross-view imagery inputs, including pre- and post-disaster street-view images, post-disaster remote sensing imagery, and street-view image pairs. Experiments show that RAPID achieves 0.92 overall accuracy for multi-disaster type classification and up to 0.627 for cross-view damage severity prediction, highlighting its potential as a foundational framework for autonomous disaster intelligence.

papers.title_en: UniverSat: Resolution- and Modality-Agnostic Transformers for Earth Observation
papers.authors: Yohann Perron, Guillaume Astruc, Nicolas Gonthier, Clement Mallet, Loic Landrieu
papers.evidence: 适用于异构遥感数据的模态无关Transformer
papers.tldr: 地球观测中传感器、模态、分辨率的多样性使得传统Vision Transformer难以直接应用。本文提出UniverSat，其通用补丁编码器将任意分辨率与模态的补丁映射到统一嵌入空间，利用自监督学习在异构多模态数据上训练单一模型。在GeoBench等基准上，该方法在分类和分割任务中取得优异表现。UniverSat首次实现了真正模态与分辨率无关的EO ViT骨干，显著提升了跨传感器泛化能力。
papers.abstract_en: Vision Transformers (ViT) dominate computer vision. However, their reliance on rigid patch projectors hinders transfer to Earth Observation (EO), where input modalities, scales, and resolutions vary widely. We introduce UniverSat, a ViT-style backbone built around a Universal Patch Encoder that maps patches from arbitrary spatial, spectral, and temporal resolutions, and from both optical and non-optical sensors, into a shared embedding space with a shared set of weights. This enables training a single model on heterogeneous multimodal corpora via self-supervision, yielding robust, sensor-agnostic spatial features. We validate this approach with strong results across classification and segmentation on standard EO benchmarks from GeoBench, PANGEABench, and SpectralEarth. Our code and models are available at https://github.com/gastruc/UniverSat.

papers.title_en: ReA-OVCD: Reliability-Aware Open-Vocabulary Change Detection via Semantic and Spatial Refinement
papers.authors: Hongming Zhu, Huaji Chen, Bowen Du, Sicong Liu, Qin Liu
papers.evidence: 多模态遥感变化检测，结合文本提示
papers.tldr: 现有开放词汇变化检测方法在实例级对比中忽略细粒度语义变化，而直接像素对比存在语义模糊和边界伪影。本文提出无训练的ReA-OVCD框架，先利用像素语义差异生成候选变化区域，再通过语义变化推理模块抑制偶然不一致，并通过边界感知细化模块验证内部像素可靠性以优化边界。在四个数据集上F1^C提升2.13%-9.75%，且计算效率更高。该方法有效平衡了变化检测的灵活性与可靠性。
papers.abstract_en: Unlike traditional remote sensing change detection that relies on predefined categories, Open-Vocabulary Change Detection (OVCD) identifies land cover changes flexibly using arbitrary text prompts. However, existing methods suffer from an inherent trade-off when modeling changes: instance-level comparison overlooks fine-grained semantic variations (e.g., partial building extensions), while direct pixel comparison proves unreliable, yielding unstable responses and boundary artifacts due to semantic ambiguity and spatial inconsistency. To this end, we propose an efficient training-free Reliability-Aware Open-Vocabulary Change Detection (ReA-OVCD) framework. It first derives candidate change regions from pixel-wise semantic discrepancies to ensure flexible and detailed localization. To ensure reliability, it subsequently introduces a collaborative refinement strategy to explicitly model change validity from both semantic and spatial perspectives. Specifically, we develop a Semantic Change Reasoning (SCR) module that reassesses changes by jointly analyzing distributional divergence and response variation, enabling the suppression of incidental inconsistencies while preserving reliable semantic shifts. In addition, a Boundary-aware Change Refinement (BCR) module is designed to mitigate artifacts stemming from boundary misalignment and uncertainty through validating whether candidate regions are supported by reliable interior pixels. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets (LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, DSIFN, and SECOND) demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, achieving $\mathrm{F}_{1}^{C}$ improvements of 2.13\% to 9.75\% with higher computational efficiency. The code is publicly available at \https://github.com/Funny0101/ReA-OVCD

papers.title_en: AEF-Econ: Toward Plug-and-Play Socioeconomic Foundation Embeddings from AlphaEarth for Urban Remote Sensing
papers.authors: Shuyang Hou, Ziqi Liu, Haoyue Jiao, Lutong Xie, Yaxian Qing, Xiaopu Zhang, Qingyang Xu, Zhangyan Xu, Xuefeng Guan, Huayi Wu
papers.evidence: 多模态自监督学习融合用于遥感社会经济任务
papers.tldr: AlphaEarth的遥感基础嵌入AEF在物理信号上表现优异，但直接用于社会经济任务效果不佳。本文通过集成人口、夜间灯光、POI等多源数据，构建CHN-Econ基准并开展31个控制实验，发现低维语义特征被高维数据抑制。提出容量自适应重建（CAR）方法，为每个数据流设置独立解码器和损失函数，缓解特征竞争。最终AEF-Econ嵌入将跨区域R²从0.301提升至0.848，跨层级从0.160提升至0.693，并恢复了负值标签。该嵌入可即插即用于社会经济遥感分析，补充AEF物理嵌入。
papers.abstract_en: AlphaEarth Foundations (AEF) unify global remote sensing foundation embeddings through multimodal self-supervised learning, but their pretraining focuses on physical land-surface signals, limiting plug-and-play use in socioeconomic tasks. We integrate seven heterogeneous data streams across 36 Chinese cities over eight years - AEF embeddings, population, nighttime lights, remote sensing indices, points of interest (POIs), urban morphology, and cross-lingual text - and construct CHN-Econ, a socioeconomic benchmark with 16 labels in three categories. We conduct 31 controlled experiments along five axes: fusion architecture, self-supervised objective, text integration, embedding dimensionality, and normalization. Used alone as a linear probe, AEF achieves R2 values of only 0.301 for cross-region and 0.160 for cross-tier evaluation. The five-axis ablated backbone improves these scores to 0.832 and 0.671, respectively, but reveals that low-dimensional semantic streams are consistently suppressed by high-dimensional streams under shared reconstruction. To address this bottleneck, we propose Capacity-Adaptive Reconstruction (CAR), replacing shared reconstruction with per-stream decoders and stream-level losses to mitigate inter-stream capacity competition. CAR further raises cross-region and cross-tier R2 to 0.848 and 0.693, and restores collapsed labels from negative R2 to a stable range. Using CAR, we infer 14.4 million pixels across 36 cities and eight years and release AEF-Econ, including 128d and 64d compressed versions. Self-diagnostics and case studies show that AEF-Econ captures cross-city hierarchies and intra-urban spatial organization under unsupervised settings, providing a socioeconomic remote sensing foundation embedding complementary to AEF physical embeddings.

papers.title_en: Neural Conjugate Aggregation: Identifiable Unsupervised Multi-Sensor Regression under Heterogeneous Sensor Bias
papers.authors: Muhammed Faruk Aytin, Zehra Demir, Alper Ünal, Julian Marshall, Gözde Ünal
papers.evidence: 无监督多传感器数据融合
papers.tldr: 在多传感器数据融合中，训练时缺乏真实标签导致监督学习不可行，且传感器存在异质性偏差。本文提出神经共轭聚合模型（NCAM），将神经网络与共轭高斯推理结合，在无监督条件下学习各传感器的偏差和可靠性，生成潜在目标变量的可解析后验，并分解认知和偶然不确定性。通过传感器锚定和方差正则化解决非可辨识性问题，并集成局部自适应蒙特卡洛共形预测，提供有限样本保证的异方差预测区间。在合成数据和真实空气质量数据集上，NCAM相比均值聚合、概率PCA和卡尔曼滤波等无监督基线，预测精度更高且不确定性校准更优。
papers.abstract_en: We study regression-based data fusion under uncertainty, where multiple noisy and biased measurement sources are available but ground-truth labels are absent during training. This setting arises in sensor networks, simulation ensembles, and scientific monitoring systems where supervision is costly or infeasible. We propose the Neural Conjugate Aggregation Model (NCAM), a hierarchical Bayesian framework that combines neural networks with conjugate Gaussian inference for unsupervised multi-source fusion. NCAM learns source-specific bias and reliability conditioned on contextual covariates, yielding an analytically tractable posterior over a latent target variable with decomposed epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty. Structural non-identifiability is resolved through sensor anchoring and variance regularization, enabling stable and interpretable posterior aggregation. To complement Bayesian uncertainty with finite-sample guarantees, we integrate locally adaptive Monte Carlo conformal prediction, producing heteroscedastic prediction intervals with coverage guarantees under exchangeability assumptions. Experiments on synthetic and real-world air-quality datasets demonstrate improved predictive accuracy and well-calibrated uncertainty compared to unsupervised baselines, including mean aggregation, probabilistic PCA, and Kalman filtering.

papers.title_en: Hedgementation = Hedgerow Segmentation: A Remote Sensing Benchmark
papers.authors: Nathan Senyard, Salem Hamdani, Astrid Zhang, Derek Wang, Evan Shelhamer, Mathias Lécuyer, Joséphine Gantois
papers.evidence: 遥感基准；多源数据融合
papers.tldr: 树篱是农业生态系统的重要组成，但缺乏国家尺度高分辨率的遥感基准。我们提出Hedgementation基准，整合Sentinel-2等多源遥感数据和法国树篱清单，构建10m²分辨率数据集。通过空间距离和气候区划的泛化测试，评估三个基线模型并对比监督与自监督方法。该基准提供标准化评估平台，开源代码推动农业精细特征追踪研究。
papers.abstract_en: We propose Hedgementation: a new benchmark to evaluate machine learning models for hedgerow mapping from remote sensing data at country scale and 10m$^2$ spatial resolution. We combine and harmonize multiple remote sensing data products and ground truth labels sourced from a hedgerow inventory in France. We measure the ability of three baseline models to generalize across spatial distance, and across climatic zones, a more explicitly challenging task. Our benchmark tests both supervised and self-supervised learning approaches for remote sensing, applied to tracking fine-scale features of high agricultural importance. The code to reproduce the benchmark and baselines results is available at https://github.com/hedgementation/hedgementation.
